claims.text: Parallel execution is gated on a per-tool boolean method `isConcurrencySafe(input)`. Default in buildTool() is false (fail-closed). Tools must opt in.

Tool calls are partitioned into batches via `partitionToolCalls()`. Consecutive concurrency-safe tools form one batch; any non-safe tool forms a singleton batch. This preserves order: parallel reads run together, writes are isolated.
Concurrent batches are run with `runToolsConcurrently()` which uses a custom `all()` utility with a concurrency cap. Default cap is 10, configurable via CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY env var.

There is also a `StreamingToolExecutor` class that starts executing tools as their ToolUseBlock arrives during streaming (before the model finishes). It uses the same isConcurrencySafe predicate but processes the queue continuously as tools stream in.

StreamingToolExecutor maintains tool status as: queued -> executing -> completed -> yielded. Results are emitted in declaration order (not completion order), which preserves the transcript's causal integrity for the model.

Context modifiers from tool results (context-mutating tool results) are deferred and applied after all parallel tools complete. They are not supported for concurrent tools (comment: 'None are actively being used').

The query loop receives tool results via an async generator. Whether using StreamingToolExecutor or runTools (non-streaming), the interface is identical: `for await (const update of toolUpdates)`. This abstracts both modes behind a common stream.
